focus: Parser-instance reuse hypothesis discrimination + upstream sweep + isolation-test reproducer

claim: parserInstance is a true module-level singleton; allocated once in ensureInit() at tree-sitter-parser.ts:87 and reused for every parse call across all files and all grammar switches
impact: establishes the lifecycle architecture as PRECONDITION for any state-corruption hypothesis

claim: caller loop is strictly sequential — for-of + await against a shared singleton; no Promise.all, no concurrency, every file in a scan traverses the same parserInstance
impact: rules out concurrent-parse race conditions; B2 is a sequential-state phenomenon, not a race

claim: isolation probes REJECT the simplest 'bash-B1 throw poisons next parse' theory — P5 (bash-throw → ts) PASSES, P4 (clean-ts → ts) PASSES, P1/P2 (alone) PASS
impact: production B2 is not a 1- or 2-file deterministic pattern; reproduction requires longer history or different mechanism

claim: vitest.phase-k.config.ts is not a standalone B2 trigger and fails to crash even after a clean-ts predecessor or a bash B1 throw — combined with P1/P2 outcomes, no single sampled OOB victim crashes deterministically in a fresh process
impact: disqualifies any 'find the toxic file' approach; cohort-replay reproducer is now mandatory if deterministic isolation is required

claim: vendored web-tree-sitter@0.24.7 is 2 minor versions and 8+ patch versions behind upstream latest 0.26.8 (queried 2026-05-06); tree-sitter-wasms@0.1.13 matches upstream latest
impact: opens a single-line-dependency-bump remediation path that may silently fix B1+B2 without touching grammar artifacts

claim: upstream tree-sitter issue tracker has zero exact-phrase hits for our crash signal; recent OOB fix tree-sitter/tree-sitter#5573 is a CLI debug pretty-printer color-array OOB, not WASM linear-memory
evidence: gh search issues 'memory access out of bounds wasm' --owner tree-sitter (0 hits)
impact: either (a) bug is fixed silently in 0.25.x/0.26.x release notes, (b) WAI per upstream contract on parserInstance reuse, or (c) build-pair-specific to 0.24.7 + 0.1.13
